content.full_text:
County of Santa Clara
Office of the County Counsel

County Government Center, East Wing
70 West Hedding Street
San Jose, California 95110-1770
(408) 299-2111
(408) 292-7240 (FAX)

Steven M. Woodside
County Counsel

July 18, 1990

Peggy Bernardy, Esq.
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Request for Advice on Behalf of Associated
Terra Consultants, Inc., Independent Contractor
to County of Santa Clara

Dear Ms. Bernardy:

As a follow-up to our telephone conversation today, I would
appreciate the Commission's advice regarding the following
question:

May a principal stockholder in a geotechnical
consulting firm, which as an independent contractor
provides geologic/geotechnical review services for
the County of Santa Clara, participate in making
decisions regarding permit processing and review
procedures for geologic/geotechnical reports
required under County ordinances.

Factual Background:

As I mentioned on the telephone, Associated Terra
Consultants, Inc., has performed geologic review services for the
County of Santa Clara since the October 17 earthquake. A
principal stockholder in that corporation, Mr. Rick Haltenhoff,
provides the bulk of those services on behalf of Associated Terra
Consultants, Inc. When Mr. Haltenhoff assumed this position for
the County, it was clearly understood that he would not review any
geologic/geotechnical reports which might be submitted by
Associated Terra Consultants, Inc. on behalf of applicants, and
would in no manner solicit business for Associated Terra
Consultants, Inc. in the course of performing his duties for the
County of Santa Clara. It is my understanding that Mr. Haltenhoff
contacted Mr. Jo Garcia, of the Fair Political Practices
Commission, and obtained a verbal assurance that his service to
the County under these conditions would not preclude Associated
Terra Consultants, Inc. from performing geologic/geotechnical
services for applicants within the County of Santa Clara.

Chief Assistant County Counsel: Ann Miller Ravel
Chief Deputies: Robert J Menifee, Susan G Levenberg, William I. Anderson

Re:    Request for Advice on Behalf of
       Associated Terra Consultants, Inc., etc.
July 18, 1990
Page Two.


       As Mr. Haltenhoff's responsibilities have evolved, and in
accordance with the description of services under his agreement
with the County, Mr. Haltenhoff has participated in making
recommendations regarding the procedures for processing and
reviewing land development applications, improvement plans, and
geologic/geotechnical reports.  It is my preliminary judgment that
his participation in making such recommendations does not have a
material financial effect on the business entity in which he has
an interest, namely, Associated Terra Consultants, Inc.  In this
respect, the recommendations regarding processing and review
procedures comprise the administrative definition of requirements
under our County Geologic Ordinance, and affect all geologic/geo-
technical consulting firms uniformly.

       There has been concern expressed to the Department of
Planning and Development regarding a possible conflict of interest
arising from Mr. Haltenhoff's services to the County.  While I
believe there are no financial effects on Associated Terra Consul-
tants, Inc. arising from his administrative duties, I would
appreciate the Commission's formal opinion as to whether or not
his role in developing recommendations regarding permit processing
and review procedures is in any manner precluded by the California
Fair Political Practices Act.  To assist your determination, I
include a copy of the latest Agreement for Services executed by
Mr. Haltenhoff on behalf of Associated Terra Consultants, Inc.  I
enclose as well a copy of the County's Geologic Ordinance, Section
C12-600 et seq. of the Ordinance Code of the County of Santa Clara.
Should any further information or documentation be helpful to the
Commission in responding to this inquiry, please do not hesitate
to contact me.

       I appreciate your taking a few moments to speak with me this
morning, as well as the Commission's assistance in resolving this
question.

       With best regards, I am

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        STEVEN M. WOODSIDE
                                        County Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        JAMES E. LEWIS, JR.
                                        Deputy County Counsel

JEL:smw
Enclosure.

sections.facts:
As I mentioned on the telephone, Associated Terra Consultants, Inc., has performed geologic review services for the County of Santa Clara since the October 17 earthquake. A principal stockholder in that corporation, Mr. Rick Haltenhoff, provides the bulk of those services on behalf of Associated Terra Consultants, Inc. When Mr. Haltenhoff assumed this position for the County, it was clearly understood that he would not review any geologic/geotechnical reports which might be submitted by Associated Terra Consultants, Inc. on behalf of applicants, and would in no manner solicit business for Associated Terra Consultants, Inc. in the course of performing his duties for the County of Santa Clara. It is my understanding that Mr. Haltenhoff contacted Mr. Joe Garcia, of the Fair Political Practices Commission, and obtained a verbal assurance that his service to the County under these conditions would not preclude Associated Terra Consultants, Inc. from performing geologic/geotechnical services for applicants within the County of Santa Clara.

Re: Request for Advice on Behalf of Associated Terra Consultants, Inc., etc.
July 18, 1990
Page Two.

As Mr. Haltenhoff's responsibilities have evolved, and in accordance with the description of services under his Agreement with the County, Mr. Haltenhoff has participated in making recommendations regarding the procedures for processing and reviewing land development applications, improvement plans, and geologic/geotechnical reports. It is my preliminary judgment that his participation in making such recommendations does not have a material financial effect on the business entity in which he has an interest, namely, Associated Terra Consultants, Inc. In this respect, the recommendations regarding processing and review procedures comprise the administrative definition of requirements under our County Geologic Ordinance, and affect all geologic/geotechnical consulting firms uniformly.

There has been concern expressed to the Department of Planning and Development regarding a possible conflict of interest arising from Mr. Haltenhoff's services to the County. While I believe there are no financial effects on Associated Terra Consultants, Inc. arising from his administrative duties, I would appreciate the Commission's formal opinion as to whether or not his role in developing recommendations regarding permit processing and review procedures is in any manner precluded by the California Fair Political Practices Act. To assist your determination, I include a copy of the latest Agreement for Services executed by Mr. Haltenhoff on behalf of Associated Terra Consultants, Inc. I enclose as well a copy of the County's Geologic Ordinance, Section Cl2-600 et seq. of the Ordinance Code of the County of Santa Clara. Should any further information or documentation be helpful to the Commission in responding to this inquiry, please do not hesitate to contact me.

I appreciate your taking a few moments to speak with me this morning, as well as the Commission's assistance in resolving this question.

With best regards, I am
